question: Does the paper report HIV sequences from patient samples?
explain: The paper mentions that patient-derived viruses were used in the study. Specifically, it states that two patient-derived M41L viruses (pM41L-1 and pM41L-2) were generated and used for in vitro selection experiments. Additionally, the study population section describes the inclusion of patients diagnosed with HIV-1 and the analysis of their baseline genotypic resistance tests.
sentences: "To compare *in vitro* development of drug resistance to tenofovir and emtricitabine, three subtype B M41L viruses were generated: two patient-derived M41L viruses (pM41L-1 and -2) and a site-directed mutant (SDM-M41L)."; "We investigated individuals who were newly diagnosed with HIV-1 in two centres in the Netherlands: University Medical Center Utrecht and Rijnstate Hospital in Arnhem."; "Of 530 patients, 279 initiated tenofovir/emtricitabine-containing first-line regimens and had ≥1 year of clinical follow-up. Of these, 31 patients were diagnosed with drug-resistant HIV-1, of which 17 patients had a variant harbouring only M41L."

question: Does the paper report in vitro drug susceptibility data?
explain: The paper includes detailed descriptions of in vitro drug susceptibility experiments. It mentions the use of a multiple cycle assay to determine the in vitro drug susceptibility of the virus panel and defines resistance as the fold increase in EC50 compared with the WT laboratory strain HXB2.
sentences: "The *in vitro* drug susceptibility of the virus panel was determined using a multiple cycle assay."; "Resistance was defined as the fold increase in EC ~50~ compared with the WT laboratory strain HXB2."; "The susceptibility of all viruses with a single M41L to tenofovir and emtricitabine was comparable to WT virus (tenofovir EC ~50~ : 1.9 μM, emtricitabine EC ~50~ : 1.6 μM in MT2 cells)."

question: Which HIV species were studied in the paper?
explain: The paper consistently refers to HIV-1 throughout the text. It mentions the study of HIV-1 patients, the generation of HIV-1 M41L variants, and the analysis of HIV-1 subtypes.

question: Which HIV genes were reported to have been sequenced?
explain: The paper focuses on the sequencing of the reverse transcriptase (RT) gene. It mentions the amplification and sequencing of the N-terminus of RT and the genotypic analysis of the entire RT gene.
sentences: "The N-terminus of RT (amino acids 25--314) of both patient-derived viruses was amplified, digested and ligated using the previously described NRT vector system."; "After five (tenofovir and tenofovir + emtricitabine) or six passages (emtricitabine), viral RNA was isolated for genotypic analysis of the entire RT gene."

question: From which countries were the sequenced samples obtained?

question: From what years were the sequenced samples obtained?
explain: The paper mentions that the baseline genotypic resistance tests were generated between 2007 and 2010.
sentences: "Inclusion criteria were as follows: (i) baseline genotypic resistance test generated between 2007 and 2010; (ii) initiation of first-line therapy with a tenofovir/emtricitabine-containing regimen; (iii) ≥1 year of clinical follow-up after therapy initiation; and (iv) detection of WT virus or a variant harbouring M41L as the only resistance mutation according to the IAS-USA list of resistance mutations."

question: What method was used for sequencing?
explain: The paper describes the use of sequence analysis to verify clones, which is indicative of Sanger sequencing. Additionally, the baseline population sequences of the HIV pol gene were aligned using ClustalW, a method commonly associated with Sanger sequencing.
sentences: "Clones were verified by sequence analysis."; "Baseline population sequences of the HIV *pol* gene from all patients included in the cohort analysis were aligned using ClustalW."
